title: Reaction Time Test
description: Test your reaction speed online for free. Click when the screen turns green. Compare your average with world rankings. No install needed.

howToUse.label: Start
howToUse.description: Click the colored area to begin. The screen will show 'Wait...' in blue.

howToUse.label: Wait
howToUse.description: Wait for the screen to turn green. The delay is random (1.5–5 seconds) to prevent cheating.

howToUse.label: Click!
howToUse.description: As soon as the screen turns green, click it as fast as you can.

howToUse.label: Check your result
howToUse.description: Your reaction time in milliseconds is shown. Under 200ms is excellent. Average human reaction time is around 250ms.

faq.question: What is a good reaction time?
faq.answer: The average human reaction time is around 200–250ms. Under 200ms is excellent; under 150ms is exceptional. Top gamers typically average 150–180ms.

faq.question: Why do I sometimes get very fast results?
faq.answer: Clicking before the green screen appears registers as 'Too Early'. This test detects and rejects early clicks to ensure accurate measurement.

faq.question: Is my best result saved?
faq.answer: Yes. Your best reaction time is saved in your browser's local storage and persists between sessions.

faq.question: What affects reaction time?
faq.answer: Factors include caffeine intake, fatigue, age, and practice. Reaction times tend to be fastest when alert and worsen with fatigue.

faq.question: How many attempts should I take?
faq.answer: Take at least 5 attempts and use the average for a reliable measurement. A single result can vary significantly due to random factors.
